title: Latches on conditions optimized as X upon multiple conditions
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
On the current master (707d98b06c8d2ad196ba64afbbb3bf5af475c75b) , on Ubuntu 20.04,

With the following *SystemVerilog* code : 
```systemverilog
module mwe (
	input  logic       i_clk   , //!
	input  logic       i_rst_n , //!
	input  logic       i_valid , //!
	input  logic       i_force , //!
	output logic [2:0] o_signal  //!
);

	always_ff @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin : p_seq
		if (~ i_rst_n ) begin
			o_signal <= 0;
		end else begin
			if (i_force) begin
				o_signal <= 0;
			end else if (i_valid) begin
				o_signal <= o_signal +1;
			end
		end
	end

endmodule
```
and using yosys with the command `read_verilog -sv mwe.sv ; proc ; opt ; show` I get the following graph, on which you can notice the `3'x` that I highlighted.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1909563/143947774-cd352a6b-f35d-4427-a811-32603c00531d.png)

## Expected behavior

I would expect the `$4 $mux` to have a latched `o_signal` value instead of the `3'x`  

## Actual behavior

`$4 $mux` have a `X` value at its input which will probably be optimized out later.

This seems to be due to the fact that `o_signal` is in two different conditions as having only the following code in the "active" (not under reset) region of `p_seq` works as expected. 
```systemverilog
if (i_valid) begin
    o_signal <= o_signal +1;
end
```
Works as expected.

A workaround, in this situation can be to use the following code, which works as expected. 
```systemverilog
if (i_force | i_valid) begin
    o_signal <= i_force ? 0 : o_signal +1;
end
```
And produce this graph: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1909563/143950021-193e3dcf-7859-4d32-8ff9-1b2105313b6a.png)
 

FWIW, this issue also reproduce using a Surelog + UHDM + Yosys setup.

Best regards
Julien FAUCHER
code_blocks:
module mwe (
	input  logic       i_clk   , //!
	input  logic       i_rst_n , //!
	input  logic       i_valid , //!
	input  logic       i_force , //!
	output logic [2:0] o_signal  //!
);

	always_ff @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin : p_seq
		if (~ i_rst_n ) begin
			o_signal <= 0;
		end else begin
			if (i_force) begin
				o_signal <= 0;
			end else if (i_valid) begin
				o_signal <= o_signal +1;
			end
		end
	end

endmodule
